utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , ms. james . nice to meet you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice to meet you , dr. cooper . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm well . hey , dragon , i'm seeing ms. james . she's a 42-year-old female , and what brings you in today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i hurt my , uh , finger when i was skiing this past weekend .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: really ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . yeah , so , um , i was going down hill , double diamonds , uh , double black diamonds , and i just lost control , and i , you know , flipped down a few ways , but , uh , somewhere along the way , i , i jammed my , my index finger on something . i'm not sure what .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so this happened last saturday , you said ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it was saturday , yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so about five days of this right index finger pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you taken any medicine for it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i took some ibuprofen . um , did n't really seem to help .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . have you iced it or put a splint on it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and what would you rate your pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's about a seven .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: a seven out of 10 pain . um , and no other injuries while you went down this double black diamond ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , interestingly enough , this is the only one .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , i guess you escaped ... i mean ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: could have been much worse .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: could have been much worse , yes . so , um , do you have any medical problems ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um ... yeah , i'm ... i have chronic constipation .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you take any medicine for it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: miralax .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and does that help ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: seems to flow nicely .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good for you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um ... have you ever had any surgeries before ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i had my appendix out when i was seven .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , let's look at your x-ray together . hey , dragon , show me the last x-ray . all right . so looking at your right hand here , and that index finger looks great . so all the joints , bones are in the right places , no fractures , so you've got a normal right hand x-ray . um , let's go ahead and check your finger out . now does it hurt when i push on your finger like this ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does it hurt when i pull your finger like this ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does it hurt when i squeeze over this joint here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . please do n't do that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does it hurt when i squeeze over this ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um ... so with your x-ray , and with your exam , looks like you have a sprain of your distar- distal interphalangeal joint . it's called your dip joint , of your right index finger , and so what we're gon na do for that is we're gon na put a splint on that right finger . i'm gon na give you a strong antiinflammatory called mobic . you'll take 15 milligrams once a day . i'll prescribe 14 of those for you . and i want you to come back and see me in two weeks , and let's make sure it's all healed up and if we need to start any hand therapy at that point , then we can . do you have any questions for me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no questions . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . hey , dragon , order the medications and procedures mentioned . and why do n't you come with me , and we'll get you checked out ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the report .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 42-year-old female.
Right index finger pain.
The patient injured her finger while skiing.
The patient jammed her index finger after falling while skiing.
The patient has had right index finger pain for five days.
The doctor asks if the patient has taken any medication for the finger pain.
The patient took ibuprofen, which did not help.
The doctor asks if the patient has iced the finger or used a splint.
The patient denies icing the finger or wearing a splint.
The doctor asks the patient to rate her pain.
The patient rates her pain as 7/10.
The doctor asks if the patient has any medical problems.
The patient has chronic constipation.
The doctor asks if the patient takes medication for constipation.
The patient takes MiraLAX.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any surgeries.
The patient had an appendectomy at age seven.
X-rays show no fractures in the right index finger.
The patient reports pain upon palpation of the finger.
The diagnosis is a right index finger DIP joint sprain.
The doctor recommends a splint for the finger.
The doctor prescribes Mobic 15 mg once a day for 14 days.
The patient will follow up in two weeks.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.

case_description: right index finger pain; chronic constipation;hx appendectomy
